utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final plan: it includes health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Next, verify if each is fully specified with required details: 1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan provides named plans for physical and mental health and their costs (€100/month and €65/month). Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides 'home_contents' with plan name and cost (€90/month). Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. Plan provides plan name and cost (€115/month). Fully specified. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan lists null, which means no services; since the format asks for 'any enhancement services with costs', having none is still a valid explicit entry but does not include services; however, the item exists and is specified as none—this counts as included because it's part of the plan with a clear value. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan provides total_monthly_cost and total_annual_cost. Fully specified. Therefore, 5 items requested, 5 present, all adequately specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes four specific items: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical health, LifeFirst Standard Plan for mental health, PrimeHome Premium Coverage for home contents, and PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel insurance. I looked up each of these in the ground-truth ratings under their respective categories and extracted the exact rating numbers.
